text:
I had purchased this previously. Despite people complaining about the pumice/lava rock, I rather liked the soap bar and the smell. So much so, that when I ran out I came right back to my order history and ordered it again. Much to my dismay the product that came was different. Gone were the exfoliating bits, and the scent, and now there was an offensive patchouli and citrus smell. I quickly logged onto the Amazon page to verify I wasn't going crazy, and low and behold the description now said "patchouli, orange and lavender" instead of "subtle notes of woods, leather, and musk". I logged onto the manufacturers website, and they still had pictures of the old bar, but their website was now updated to the new scent as well.

I wouldn't mind if they changed their product, but I feel like going to a polar opposite (subtle wood, leather and musk to strong offensive patchouli, orange and lavender) experience is borderline bait and switch.

text:
(Ingredients list is at the bottom of this review.)

I am really loving the TINAZANA Vegan Bakuchiol Comforting Cream (2.7oz / 80ml). Only 9 ingredients. It is so good. Truly comforting and non-irritating. Even when my skin is already in an irritated state.

This is a beautifully thick, rich, white moisturizing cream. Yet, somehow it feels decently lightweight and comfortable on the face and neck. Not lightweight like a gel cream, by any means. But comfortable. This cream glides onto the face and neck smoothly, and it soaks into the skin quickly; But it doesn't disappear. It leaves a lasting, soothing, comforting layer of moisture and hydration on the surface of the skin. It helps to lock-in the moisture and hydration from the previous steps in my skincare routine, and leave my skin feeling so lovely and comfortable.

The scent is extremely slight. It is herbal, but it's almost undetectable, unless you're really seeking after the scent.

The packaging is a beautiful yellow glass jar with a beige twist-off lid. It feels very nice in the hand, and the cream feels luxurious on the face. Much more expensive than it is. The 80ml size is excellent for the price.

The addition of gentle, anti-aging Bakuchiol is also a big win with zero irritation. Plus, Shea Butter for maximum moisture. I layer gentle, hydrating, soothing, calming toners and serums underneath this cream, and I get excellent glowy, dewy results.

I use this cream day and night, as I have dry, sensitive skin. I imagine many skin types might like this for day or night, especially during colder months.

If you have sensitive skin, and you cannot use most skincare products (like me), this is likely going to be for you.

Overall, I'm highly impressed. Why did it take me so long to find this? This cream is so beautifully simple. It's truly an amazing find for someone like me, who struggles with sensitive skin. I will definitely repurchase, and I'm looking forward to trying more simple, gentle products from Tinazana.

I highly recommend this cream to anyone and everyone.

INGREDIENTS:

Water, Butyrospermum Parkii (Shea) Butter, Butylene Glycol, 1,2-Hexanediol, Polysilicone-34, Isononyl Isononanoate, Caprylic/Capric Triglyceride, Ethyhexylglycerin, Bakuchiol.

text:
I hate the soaking process. My hands end up freezing and I just don't have the patience to sit for as long as it takes to soak my gel polish off. So then I end up using a file to remove it - it works but it is very time consuming and at least my hands are "doing" something. Plus, sometimes I go a little overboard and end up with thinning out my actual nail. Oops! And owie!

This is where Gel Nail Polish Remover comes in. Before buying this product I read many, many reviews on various similar products as well as this one. The "worst" reviews I saw complained of this product causing nails to curl. As it so happens, exactly as I thought, the cause of nails curling from this product is THIN NAILS. I had previously gone a little rough on one of my pinky nails, thinning it out to the point that putting pressure on the nail hurt. Of course, that nail curled up when I applied this product. My other nails, all still mostly standard thickness, were fine. Also, it is very important to note that the nail does relax back to normal shape after removal. At least, mine did.

I had also read in another review the suggestion to apply one coat, leave on for 5 or 6 minutes then apply another coat. I can concur that this is the best practice. It is also important to remember to user your nail file to rough up/remove the top coat. If you forget that step, it won't do a darned thing!

I have now used this product no less than 3 times, on both my hands and feet, and can say that the nail curling only happened on the first application. It has not happened again with additional polish removal sessions. Again, it is important to not skip the filing off of the top layer and to apply multiple times, allowing ample time for the product to work between applications.

I absolutely recommend this product just so long as you fully understand the limitations and HOW to use it. Great value for the price, too!

text:
This review is for people who are trying to decide on the Sonicare or a Braun Oral B (or ANY spin brush for that matter). My previous toothbrush was an Braun Oral B, and in my experience, the Sonicare is way superior.

First of all, the Oral B has a rotating head. Well, after several uses, I've noticed that the head starts getting loose and my lips and cheeks sometimes get pinched in the gap between the head and the neck of the brush. This causes me to have to change the brush even before it's due. Also, it seems that the Oral B allows a lot of water to enter into the metal parts that make the head spin. Therefore, while brushing, the water along with toothpaste seem to clean those parts which results in dirty water in your mouth! It's not pretty sight rinsing out your mouth and toothbrush and seeing grey water in the sink!

So after dealing with spin brushes for several years, I decided to give the Sonicare a try. Besides all the hassles of the spin type brushes, most importantly, the Sonicare cleans better. I was surprised at how well it performed since I thought the Oral B was a huge step above a manual brush. But the Sonicare makes my teeth feel even *cleaner* and I love how it makes my gums feel as well! When the Sonicare first came out in the market, I knew that there was no way the head would even fit in my small mouth (with all my wisdom teeth in tact!) so that's why I never considered it before. However, the current design of the brushes is fantastic. They are compact and angled and I have no problems reaching all my teeth. I have a feeling once you try the Sonicare, you'll never go back to any other toothbrush. It's really *that* great!

Update 05Oct2020:
I used the lavender bath bombs last night and remembered to take pictures of the water this time. The lavender bomb really did make my water a very pretty purple. When I used the eucalyptus bath bomb, it really did my water a really pretty blue green color. My tub didn’t stain, nor did my bath billow. And since I got rid of the horrendous item that I kept having to mess with that caused me to make the pillow keep sliding, the pillow stayed put this time. I HIGHLY recommend this product!

I received this two days ago, 19 September 2020, and I put it to use yesterday 20 Sept 2020. I’m really happy with this purchase. My initial impression was very positive. It really did look like it came straight out of a spa. It was squishy and felt comfortable. The bathbombs were a great touch. And it came with a very large mesh bag so that you can wash in there and not ruin it (I think, that’s what that is for). When I used it yesterday, it really did feel comfortable and there were no pressure pains. It did slide a bit down, but was easy to slide back up into position (I think it was also sliding because I kept having to adjust another item not from this purchase). The bathbomb that I used yesterday was the eucalyptus one. The fragrance in the box was nice, once I opened it, the fragrance was more subtle. It turned my bath water blue! It looked really nice but then I was thinking it was going to stain my tub blue. After I drained my tub there were no stains (phew!). The actual bath pillow was very easy to rinse off and hang up to dry. It was overall a very pleasant experience. The way this came, would make a great gift for someone. I know I would LOVE to have received this as a gift.

i received this two days ago, 19 september 2020, and i put it to use yesterday 20 sept 2020. i’m really happy with this purchase. my initial impression was very positive. it really did look like it came straight out of a spa. it was squishy and felt comfortable. the bathbombs were a great touch. and it came with a very large mesh bag so that you can wash in there and not ruin it (i think, that’s what that is for). when i used it yesterday, it really did feel comfortable and there were no pressure pains. it did slide a bit down, but was easy to slide back up into position (i think it was also sliding because i kept having to adjust another item not from this purchase). the bathbomb that i used yesterday was the eucalyptus one. the fragrance in the box was nice, once i opened it, the fragrance was more subtle. it turned my bath water blue! it looked really nice but then i was thinking it was going to stain my tub blue. after i drained my tub there were no stains (phew!). the actual bath pillow was very easy to rinse off and hang up to dry. it was overall a very pleasant experience. the way this came, would make a great gift for someone. i know i would love to have received this as a gift.

text:
I'm blown away by how this product was able to hold up to the abuse I put it through, as a martial artist. Plus, it's healthier for my nails.

Advantages:
-Great for nail health.
-Amazingly durable.
-Short drying time.
-Gorgeous color (if you take time to mix a couple of shades together) with a nice shine.
-Dr.'s Remedy nail polish remover works surprisingly well, and requires very little to do the job.

Drawbacks:
-Very expensive. ...very expensive. Minus one star for extremely high price.
-Colors aren't exactly what I want, so I had to buy several shades and mix them to get a nice color. (yet another blow to my wallet.)

The drying time for this polish is short and durability is incredible. I am able to kick targets with my beautifully painted toenails about 45 minutes after applying all of these coats. The only other brand that has held up to this kind of abuse that I've tried is Sally Hansen, but Sally Hansen is not as good for nail health and required at least a day of curing time before kicking targets, plus it would rub off on the targets. Dr.'s Remedy doesn't rub off.

I use the base coat, then nail color, then top coat...yes, at around $17 per bottle, it was a ton of money to spend on something I was uncertain of.

I remove this polish after a week, and allow at least a day of rest for my nails.

text:
UPDATE AS OF 03/16/2019 All the previous info still applies. Now that I am 16 days out from using this product, I want to add that my feet are STILL peeling. I have been using this type (not this particular) product for a couple of years and the entire process is always completed at least by day 12. Thus, besides the horrible odor, this product is not particularly efficient at exfoliating. So, basically there is NOTHING about it that I can recommend.

No matter how good these are at exfoliating, I cannot recommend them due to the offensive/clingy/cloying/LONGLASTING odor. I have used products like this before and there is always some odor during use...but these, well, days later after soaking my feet in baking soda water and then vinegar water, the incredibly floral+chemical odor still wafts around my head--yes, all the way from my feet! It is sickening, offensive,, and embarrassing when I am around other people. Also, the socks I put on after using this product have been washed in hot water and laundry detergent and they are just going to have to be thrown away because the odor DOES NOT come out. I threw away second set of these bootees because I cannot subject myself to the odor again no matter how badly I want my feet to be soft without rough skin. DO NOT BUY if you are sensitive to odors and/or simply do not like strong odors wafting around you for days on end.

My fingernails have five coats total of base, polish and topcoat, so it is a bit of work to remove my nail polish irrespective of what I use for remover.  This remover did as good of a job as traditional nail polish remover at clearing all of those layers from my nails. text:
I am a fan of MARLOW products...I have several others not included in this set and all are great. I'll break down each product briefly below.

BAG: Very nice, basic black but well padded and holds it's shape. I basic in shape but I higher quality then usual.

SOAP: Great balsam/fir scent. Excellent balance of exfoliation and suds. Leaves body invigorated, soft and smelling great!

SHAVE: Has a tea scent, hint of some sort of alcohol. Builds into a creamy texture, not much sud but decent coverage. Does it's job. Finishes with a clean scent.

FACE WASH: Nice balsam/fir scent to it. A little goes a long way! Very slick, seems to not penetrate but wash the exterior of skin. Takes some work to wash off. Finishes with a green tea scent and refreshed.

FACE MOISTURIZER: Has a tea/alcohol scent at first...Goes on smooth. Dries fairly quickly, leaving a sweet green tea scent. Leaves face feeling soft not oily.

POMADE: Great scent, more cologne like. Goes on easy, not too tacky or slick. Does a decent job.

LIP BALM: Awesome balm!!! At first I thought it was an odd addition to the set. But man I love this balm. Goes on with a hint of sweet tea. Not waxy, not glossy, just a perfect balance that keeps lips moisturized.

text:
This is a really great primer! I have used many over the years and this is one of the top preforming primers I have ever used. It does it's job just as well as way more expensive brands.

It is for sure comparable to Smashbox Photo Ready Primer. The texture is very very similar, and it goes on just as smooth as that brand.

I use it after my morning facial routine of cleansing, serums, toning and moisturizer. I squeeze about two pumps worth onto my hands and apply it all over my face. I concentrate on areas that need filling in, like around my eyes and mouth where I have fine lines. Then let it settle for about 5 minutes and apply my normal makeup routine. My foundation glides right over it and doesn't settle in to any of my fine lines or wrinkles.

Overall this is a solid choice for a good primer. It fills in where I need it to and lasts all day. I highly recommend!

text:
I wish I'd taken pictures. As soon as I put this on my hair, I could see the difference between this and other products. I have colored my brown VERY fine, VERY thick split-personality hair (curly under, straight on top) for years ranging from a bright burgundy red to black, to various browns. This last time, I had used a bleaching prep product to add a few vibrant blue streaks. But all I got was black that won't go away.

SMELL

I've tried bleach and non-bleach products. Those smelled terrible. This didn't have much smell in comparison. It definitely didn't smell like the other reviews.

COMPARISON TO OTHER PRODUCTS

The other products I tried destroyed my hair leaving it crunchy and damaged. They also only managed to lighten the color from the crown and left the near black almost exactly as I started. (Beware of the competitor non-bleach product that says it only takes 20 minutes. The box doesn't say you have to rinse it a MINIMUM of 20 minutes. That one also required a recolor, which stabilized the hair. I think my hair would have snapped off had I not recolored it.)

COST EFFECTIVE

My hair is so thick, I had to use 2 boxes of other products just to get coverage. This product went on so easy, my hair sucked it in and softened as it went on. I only used 2/3 of the solutions in this product. I'm impressed that I didn't need it all. (Hairdressers always charged more to color my hair because it's so thick.)

PROCEDURE/OUTCOME

I left it on for probably 40 minutes, used a cap, but didn't heat. The top lightened to my natural light brown color (I forgot what that looked like!) and the black is now a rich brown, not even really a two-tone, which I've had since my experiment. But, it still needs another treatment to get it the rest of the way. My hair has blond overtones now, not brassy at all.

BONUS!

My hair is really, really soft. I would do this as a conditioning treatment! I am ordering a second batch to retreat since what I have left isn't enough. Next time, I will heat my hair slightly to see if it will take it the rest of the way. Anything left over will be used for a future treatment to condition my hair. (I live next to the ocean and the salt and wind has been really hard on my hair so I'm very happy to find something to help.)

text: I've ordered this product many times, and have always enjoyed using it.  When you dampen your face and apply the product, it foams up nicely and is a good cleanser.  The last time I ordered this, I put the bottle away in the cupboard, since I still had some cleanser from a previous purchase left to use.  When it came time to open this one recently, it was like I had a completely different product. The liquid dispensed more like a gel, with the consistency of liquid handwashing soap. Don't stock up on this product.

text:
When I first saw this, I thought this would be the answer to my hair-drying prayers. Big mistake. The first time I attached this to my Conair dryer, it got so hot that it actually burned my skin. And I mean BURNED it. When my husband tried to remove it, it broke into pieces. A few days later, my hairdresser asked what had "fried" my hair. "If it burned your skin," she said, "imagine what it did to your hair."
Then the fun began with the vendor. I filed a request to return it, and they sent me 2 screens of legalese doubletalk about how they weren't the manufacturer, only the seller, so they weren't responsible, so they wouldn't refund my money. I mean, the word "Unbreakable" is in the name of the product - but when it broke during normal use, mayabeauty didn't see a problem with that! If this isn't a defective product, what is? Be aware that anything you buy from this seller is strictly 'buyer beware' - don't expect them to stand behind it. This product will burn you - and so will the seller.

then the fun began with the vendor. i filed a request to return it, and they sent me 2 screens of legalese doubletalk about how they weren't the manufacturer, only the seller, so they weren't responsible, so they wouldn't refund my money. i mean, the word "unbreakable" is in the name of the product - but when it broke during normal use, mayabeauty didn't see a problem with that! if this isn't a defective product, what is? be aware that anything you buy from this seller is strictly 'buyer beware' - don't expect them to stand behind it. this product will burn you - and so will the seller.

text:
I am not a beautician or barber, just someone with 2 dogs needing regular clipping. I remember seeing this product or a similar one in barbershops where my Dad would go to get a haircut. It was in a good-sized flatish round jar and the barber would dip the tip and bottom of his clippers in it while running and they would come out shiny and hair free. Now the jar looked like a pot of disease waiting to get passed on to the next customer as it had floating bits of hair and residue from who knows what.

I had been cleaning and oiling my clipper blades after each grooming and using a coolant on them during clipping to keep the blade's surface from getting too hot. Cleaning the blades afterwards never seemed to get all the hair from between the teeth even using a tooth brush and sliding the two blades back and forth. This stuff really CLEANS all the hair off and you can hear that the blades are better lubricated by the reduced noise. I do wipe them quickly so as not to drip any of the H42 onto the dog. They are shining and humming pleasantly after dipping them into this stuff. I will continue to buy this product as I feel it will extend the life of my clippers and make for a better cutting blade.

Now for the down side and why I took off one star. This stuff still comes in the good sized flatish round "tub-like" container and is seems intended that you dip the blades (not the motor, just the blade tips and bottom) into the whole container full and continue to reuse this stuff over multiple cuts. I don't care if it is Virucidal and Anti-bacterial, that seems like a really unclean way to use this. I solve this problem by pouring a small amount into another container (at least 1/4 inch deep) and dip my clippers in that. I discard the stuff after each groom. Unfortunately the container it comes in does not pour without dripping and yes, you use more of this stuff using my method. I continue to use oil and coolant, but this stuff truly cleans off the bits that get between the teeth and seems to get the blades running much smoother. I do recommend this product, but not for using the reservoir full repeatedly over several cuts or clippings. That method just doesn't sound like a healthy way to go. So if you use clippers on humans or dogs, I feel this product may be of use to you.

Recommended.

text:
If I have a choice between a plain headband and a unicorn headband, well, I’m picking the unicorn headband every time!

These headbands are super cute and colorful. The faux fur is soft and doesn’t shed, and really adds to the unicorn aspect. The horns stand up from the headband, and are sparkly silver. The ears don’t stand up as well, they’re a bit floppy, and also have sparkly silver on the backs.

The headbands are on the smaller side. I measured approximately 6 inches across the opening with a headband unstretched, and about 9 inches when fully stretched. I like that the headbands are tight, they stay on my head and hold my hair back. I don’t have to keep fussing with them, they stay in place. They are about 2½ inches wide, which helps with comfort.

text:
I'v tried several types of products to remove facial hair: coil groomer, tweezers, electric face trimmer, and these. The coil groomer was the most difficult to use. No matter how much I twisted it, I could only get a hair or two after a few tries. Tweezing was faster and more effective. The electric face trimmer was designed for removing all facial hair, including nose and ear hair, so the cutting tip was very small and the cutting mechanism was gentle on delicate skin. It was great at removing the peach fuzz on my cheeks; I just needed to make several swipes to get my whole cheek.

These shavers are well made. Unfortunately, the instructions were all written in either Japanese or Chinese. I figured they looked self explanatory, so I didn't look up videos on how to use them. The cutting tip is long, which should make shaving go faster as it can cover a larger area. However, the larger blade also means that it is more difficult to reach certain areas. I have a lot of small moles on my face, so I made sure to be extra careful while using them. Despite this, I managed to nick myself a few times. What's ironic is that the nicks weren't on any moles.

I think my skin is just too delicate and uneven to use these shavers.

text:
WHAT IT COMES WITH
Nothing extra, just the shampoo.

THE REVIEW
I've been using the product for a week or two solid now, and honestly I really do enjoy it. It has no odor that I find off-putting, quite the opposite, I can actually smell the ingredients in it, especially the vanilla and honey. The fact that there are no parabens or strong chemicals in the shampoo is also a huge plus for me since I try to be environmentally friendly. My hair feels a bit bouncier and fluffier than normal, and I have thin hair so that is pretty huge to me to have that little bit of extra volume.

OVERALL
I really enjoy this product, although for the $15 price tag I'd be a little cautious. I don't have particularly damaged hair, just thin hair that I like to take good care of. $15 is a pretty steep price for a 3 month supply of shampoo, at least for me. I definitely recommend the product, it's great though. 5/5

text:
I have been using this style combs for about a decade and absolutely love them. I do not like to have a lot of different holders in my hair. You know, when you need an up-do and it ends up being a rubber band, a barrette and four bobby pins. For me it is too much. And not only to put them in, but especially to take them out at the end of the day.

Well, this comb can hold shoulder length and longer hair in a high knot all by itself. I do not use it for french twist, but rather twist hair into a bun on top of my head and insert the comb front to back. It stays put all day. I can swim, garden, clean for hours and it will not unravel. Also, it is almost unnoticeable.

these particular combs seem to be slightly better quality than the ones I bought last time. There are no chips or catches on plastic and they are higher polish. It seems to me that they will be durable. All combs I bought before last for a couple months each, which is fine since the price is so low. Highly recommend especially for situations when you need to put hair up or down without a mirror (beach, pool, gym etc). Once you learn how to do it - piece of cake....

Once in a medical facility they requested that I remove everything from my hair. The tech was astounded when I just took out this comb and a perfect topknot instantly fell down. Not only did she examine the comb and learned where to order, she also asked permission to show it to her colleagues.

Highly recommend.

text:
[[VIDEOID:bde866136544c24b41b9800127851a48]] These come with the 2 jars in a little bubble wrap bag. Inside the jars are held together with some strong plastic sealing (see photos). I really liked that because it helped ensure the jars didn’t break or the lids didn’t come loose and end up with powder everywhere! So I loved that!

The jars are plastic. On the lids is a sticker with the brand name, it’s placed on there unevenly so probably done by hand. The lids twist off and there’s a protective seal on the jars inside. Be VEEEERRRYYY careful when you pull off the protective seals. They are easy to lift and pull off, but I ended up dropping one of the seals after I took it off and it got pigment all over (see photos). I don’t have nails, which probably would have helped me hold onto them. In hindsight I should have used tweezers to pull them off. But, no big deal! The jars are both look to be filled up pretty evenly and the jars are pretty full.

These are beautiful! The shift is definitely more noticeable with the teal one than it is with the purple, but they are still both really beautiful. The shifts I see with the teal are the dark teal color, a brighter teal, dark purple, a pinky purple and a dark green. The shifts I see with the are the purple color, a royal blue color, a brighter blue, and a pinky purple.

These are loose pigments. I touched my finger on the jar to pick some up and swiped it onto my arm. It goes on pretty thin and then when I try to build it up, that only works so well....if I put a swipe on then add another swipe it builds up okay. But, if I try to add more swipes than that, it kind of moves the pigment around and I end up getting uneven areas. When you try to apply them with a brush, a brush picks the pigment up really well, but when you go to apply it, it just kind of blends it away. The more you try to add, the more it seems to blend away. But, since these are pigments, they really should be applied to a sticky base. I always use NYX Multitasker Mixing Medium as glitter glue, but really it’s to use to mix in with loose pigments. So, I took a very tiny dot of the NYX Multitasker Mixing Medium and put that onto my arm. Then, I took a brush and picked up a small amount of pigment. I applied the pigment I picked up with the brush onto the area I put the NYX Mixing Medium. The pigment stuck to it really easily and made it look beautiful - smooth, pigmented. The NYX Multitasker Mixing Medium is just a clear gel like consistency. You get a sticky area wherever you apply it. So, you don’t have to use a mixing medium. Anything that leaves a sticky area for the pigment to grab onto will work, like glitter glue, cream eyeshadow, concealer - just apply it while the product is still wet. I show a photo of the teal where I applied it on the NYX Multitasker Mixing Medium, on a white cream eyeshadow, and on a concealer (LA Girl Pro Conceal in Fairest). I also have a photo of the purple where I applied it on the NYX Multitasker Mixing Medium, on a white cream eyeshadow, and on a concealer (LA Girl Pro Conceal in Fairest). They all held onto the pigment perfectly and the pigment looked beautiful! I also tried all 3 as bases with the pigment on my eyes and they worked just as well. They lasted all day without fading. I definitely want to try more colors!

Overall, I think these are beautiful multichrome pigments! They have beautiful shifts. As I said, I can see the shifts in the teal one a lot easier than in the purple one, but that’s usually the case when I have dark purple multichromes - for me, it always seems like it’s harder to tell the different shifts in a dark purple. In my opinion, most multichromes are usually pretty pricey. At the time of this review, these 2 multichrome pigments were $25, so that’s $12.50 a piece. Considering you get a little jar full of pigment, I don’t think that’s too bad. For example, a JD Glow multichrome eyeshadow (pressed in a pan) is $15. I don’t mind paying more for an eyeshadow over a pigment because I just prefer working with eyeshadows pressed into a pan rather than loose pigments. So, for these jars to end up being $12.50 each, I don’t think that’s too bad. They look beautiful and perform well!

text:
This machine is all right if you like to shave three times a day. Otherwise, stick with your blade.

The famous pivoting heads do not actually pivot much, so upper neck, jaw and chin are a real challenge to trim. And two hours later, you feel like you need a shave.

Also, the elaborate cleaning/oiling recommended by the User's Manual will have you longing for the simplicity of your razor.

Further, the cost of replacing heads is a hidden cost. Also, the need to purchase the oil and cleaning supplies. They do not come with the machine.

Finally, the User's Manual (even though labeled 9160 XL) does not square with the machine supplied.

Bottom line: If you're thinking of switching from razors to electric shavers, think again.

text:
The only reason this razor works--and the only reason I rated it with even 3 stars--is because it's a Venus razor. Venus razors work excellently, so when this new version came out I was excited to try it. I've owned mine for over a year now, and was unimpressed with it from the moment I started using it.

The vibrations are useless; I get the same quality shave whether it's turned on or off. The battery lasted quite a long time, but as soon as I replaced it the razor stopped working properly (not that it mattered, since it didn't do anything in the first place). So not only do we have a "power" razor that has no power, but the company decided to charge even more for replacement razor cartridges. I thought I had to use the corresponding cartridges, but finally I inserted one of the old Venus cartridge heads and it worked exactly the same.

I don't know--maybe I'm just a no-fuss girl who really doesn't notice subtle differences in the quality of leg-shaving. I would recommend Venus brand razors in a heartbeat, but I do NOT recommend this particular version.

text: I'll rate this 4 stars because nothing is perfect. I have tried various electric razors and generally I am left unsatisfied. Then about a year ago I came across the Norelco 6735 Advantage razor and I am happy to report that I have found the closest thing to a perfect electric razor. The Norelco does very well with daily shaves but doesn't do well against multiple day growth. If I go more than a day without shaving, I'll use a blade to get the close shave. I don't know anything about the mirror since that was not available when I made my original purchase but the razor itself is very good. After 1 year's use, I am still using the same blades that originally came with the razor and it is still shaving as close as when I purchased it. The gels are good though I feel they are somewhat expensive. I will be purchasing 3 more of these razors for my 3 sons this Christmas and believe me, if I wasn't satisified with this razor, that wouldn't be happening.

text: I have been on a quest to find a product to replace my beloved FX Curls Up Curl Reactivator and Defrizzant since it has been discontinued with no hope of coming back.  I even went so far as to pay $45 for a bottle that used to cost $5 at Walmart.  I really didn't want to waste money trying different things so I started reading tons of reviews and narrowed it down to three different products to try.  This was the first one.  For reference I have blonde fine hair that curls pretty nicely when scrunched and dried with a diffuser.  It's still curly if I air dry but it would be kind of flat.  It also frizzes in humidity.  But my curls are very delicate I would say.  I belive they are referred to as "cherub curls".  Today was my first attempt with this product and for the most part I like it.  It is a creme whereas my FX Curls up was a serum but it was light and it didn't make my curls crunchy. My hair came out full bodied and the curls look good.  They are pretty soft.  It actually came similar to how it does with my favorite product. The only thing I noticed was that it felt a tiny bit dry where the FX would always make it almost like a wet curl that didnt dry hard.  Its hard to explain but anyway I think I can live now without my beloved FX Curls up if I have this.  I would say if you need a stronger hold maybe try one of Miss Jessie's other products.  Because this is a light hold made for finer hair.

text:
I've been searching for products that I can use for my unique skin concerns - my skin is sensitive, and I'm starting to notice visible signs of aging like dark circles, uneven skin tone, extremely dry winter skin, acne prone chin, and scarring due to a disease called HS.

I'm always happier to use stuff that is all-natural, organic, and cruelty-free, and I prefer to buy from smaller companies if possible. This serum is definitely worth the money - you get 6 oz, which will last a while! The formula is light and gentle, doesn't irritate my skin, and I can use it on every part of my face, chin and neck! I am not breaking out, and I've been using this product for a few weeks now. My skin definitely looks brighter, feels smoother and is well-moisturized.

I love products with AHA's - hyaluronic acid is an example of an Alpha-Hydroxy acid, and these ingredients are especially effective at aiding the shedding of old skin - which reveals a healthier complexion underneath those layers! This is an excellent serum and I've incorporated it into my skin care routine! If you're like me and you're looking for good stuff, look no further! Your skin will thank you! And you can feel good about your purchase because Skin2Spirit is a good company!

Also, I really loved the sample of the eye cream - it definitely helps wake up my eyes, though my sister ended up taking the sample because she also loves it!

DISCLAIMER:
I was provided this product for free or at a discounted rate for the purposes of trying it out and giving my honest feedback. This doesn't mean I'm going to give a biased opinion about the product, and I vow to always tell the truth no matter if I purchased a product or not.

As a savvy consumer, I'm not easily sold on most things, so before I make a purchase, I do my research. The opinions of other consumers are a huge factor in whether or not I end up making a purchase. I don't BS because I don't like to be BS'd and I can always tell when somebody is making a sales pitch to me – but I am a hard sell! That said, I hope my review comes across as genuine, because that's always my intention!

text:
Everyone's experience with a razor is different. I've tried a lot of different types, and I was more than happy to let go of my Gillette Fusion Power early last year. I bought a Shick Hydro Power. I thought it was great, cheap all around with an amazing cut. I even have the highest rated review on the product on Amazon currently. Now, I looked at the reviews for this saying Hydro 5 is better. It might be better for one person or another, but I have used both extensively and like this one better.

I have been using the Proglide POWER (yes, power) version for about 6-7 months now. I just purchased this manual version to take with me to basic training (in case the power version would be confiscated). I'll backtrack and talk about my experience with the Hydro 5. When I used it at first, it was kind of rough. I figured it would get a little better and it did, but it still was uncomfortable. I fooled myself into liking it because of its price and how long the lubrication strip lasted. I was getting only a week on my Fusion Power before it started to become uncomfortable because the strip faded out, and I was getting 3-4 weeks with the Hydro 5 until it ran out. The lubrication on that thing is funny though, because sometimes the whole thing will fall out of the razor.

My experience with the Proglide has been positive in every way as soon as I took it out of the box. I have learned long ago to not hype up any product until I've used it for a long time. With 6-7 months of experience I'm telling everyone how it is. The proglide can cut through thick stubble as good as it does on your 5 o clock shadow. I hadn't shaved for about a week and used the Proglide and it cut me down smooth with no pain and with ease. I bought myself a replacement pack of 8 cartridges and I have run through 6. That means in 6 months, I have used 6. Actually 7 if you include the one it came with. For those of you out there who know how to make a razor last, one of these can go up to a month or two. For beginners or average shavers, you will get at least two weeks worth before you even consider changing it, and, the lubrication will still be there.

One thing I hated about the Hydro 5 was the grip. It was a horrible design. With the Proglide, it was made with you in mind. It just feels natural holding it, and every stroke feels just right. As we all know, replacing Gillette cartridges can be a pain in the neck (not physically). To buy a pack of replacements, you're looking at about $30 for a pack of 8. The same will cost you under $20 with the Hydro 5. Not everyone cares to pay for it, but it's more than worth it. It's the best shave I ever had in my life, and I don't mind paying $10 extra every 6-9 months for a better experience.

Think of razors like gas, and you are the car. You only require regular gas, but if you decide to go with the premium (Proglide), your car will run faster and be more responsive.

I will state again I have not used the manual proglide yet, but it's the same thing without vibration (which I don't use much anyway).

Hopefully your experience is as good as mine.

Here is a run down of stats in my own opinion for the Proglide.

Fusion Proglide
Standard: $9.74 (comes with 2 cartridges)- replacement pack of 8 - $28.99
Power: $10.94 (1 cartridge) - replacement pack of 8 - $31.99

Comfort level: 5/5
Effectiveness of each stroke: 4.5/5 (sometimes you have to run the blade back over)
Smoothness of shave: 5/5 - Your experience may be different, but this is the best shave of my life
Cartridge life: 4/5 - Depends on how you use it. Will get you 2 weeks minimum and up to 2 months maximum
Design: 5/5 - The thing I hate about most razors is the design. This one feels great in hand

Overall: 4.8/5 - It's not perfect, but the best razor I've ever used in my life

It comes out of box with a razor holder which most gillette razors have, but others don't. Also, I strongly recommend buying the Proglide shaving cream. it's the best I ever used in my life. You can literally see your hairs rising in seconds if you look closely.

Good luck everyone!

It occurred to me that I was turning into the doctors retirement fund because nothing ever changed, it was the some drill over and over with no end in sight. Now I don't have any of those problems anymore with my skin or making expensive appointments and buying expensive meds.
The Readers Digest version of my story is, I found a GREAT PRODUCT by going to GREAT DOCTOR with old time values.

text:
If you haven't gotten a silicone baking mat yet, and you use your oven AT ALL, you should really get this!

It's incredibly easy to clean up. It doesn't matter whether it's those cookies that always stick to your favorite cookie sheet or a gooey cheesy mess, it really does simply wipe off.

Because it's so easy to clean, I was also able to get some of the best parts of one of my old standby recipes to come out. I like wrapping thin cut chicken breast around some kind of cheese and other fillings and baking it. The problem with this is that some of the cheese inevitably leaks out. Even on glass cookware, this is wasted food that I just can't get back in an edible form. With this mat? Nice crispy melted cheese fried in the oils that came out of the chicken breast. One of the tastiest parts of the dish, and a new favorite!

Cleaning up the dish above? I cooked it on a 1/4 sheet raised lip cookie sheet, with no prep other than putting the baking mat down. After pulling the food out, all it took to clean was an easy job wiping the mat off and then just basic washing to both the mat and the pan. While some of the juices from the chicken did get under the mat, NONE of the solids did and it was just simple to do.

The only downsize to this mat that I can find is it's size. I want a bigger one, because I don't want to keep using cooking sprays and parchment paper. It's perfectly sized for a 1/4 sheet baking sheet, but that's really what I consider a 'small' sheet unless I'm in the RV where my oven will only barely even fit this mat.

I received this product at a discount in exchange for my unbiased review.
I've been looking for reasons to use the oven for this

text:
First, I have to echo the other reviewer's sentiment about the picture being misleading. The text does state that you're buying a single stick, but the picture makes it look like you're buying a box of 'em.

I was initially disappointed with my [[ASIN:B002A5OLHQ Arko Shaving Soap Stick]]. I prefer using a brush and mug, so I unwrapped the stick, cut it into shaving soap coins (which I believe are legal tender in Venezuela and Costa Rica, and certain counties in Arkansas), put them into my mug and stuck it in the microwave for 30 seconds to melt them. What a mistake! That's what I do with [[ASIN:B001A3DNY6 Van Der Hagen Deluxe Shave Soap, 2.5-Ounce Boxes (Pack of 12)]], and it works great--the soap liquifies, adheres to the bottom and sides of the mug, and once it resolidifies, I'm good to go. This stuff softened, yes, but it didn't really liquify. Instead, it kind of foamed up and over the top of the mug. I tried to use my knife to moosh it back into the mug, but it resulted in a very rough, craggy top surface. Figured I'd just use the brush on it anyway--eventually I would wear down that rough surface and leave behind a smooth top that would work perfectly.

I'm glad I did! This soap lathered up like nobody's business when I put the warm, moist brush to it. It made a little thinner lather than I usually get from VDH, but despite it's thinness, it wasn't runny at all--it stayed right where I brushed it, and didn't dry out before my razor got to it. My habit is to do three shaves in different directions, and the Arko lather held out for all three.

I used an older blade, an [[ASIN:B001QY8QXM 100 Astra Superior Premium Platinum Double Edge Safety Razor Blades]], probably about two shaves past when I would usually have replace it, but I was being lazy. I kind of expected to nick myself a couple times. But with the Arko shaving soap, that did not happen. My blade glided across my face as smoothly as I could ask. No nicks at all.

As many have mentioned, the scent is kind of strong, and not entirely pleasant. Some have compared it to citronella, which I can understand. I though it was kind of a cross between the original Old Spice scent from when my dad used their cologne, and some industrial chemical smell. VDH is more or less unscented, so I usually mix a few drops of my favorite cologne ([[ASIN:B00AATHC52 ASPEN by Coty]]) into the liquid when it comes out of the microwave, but I didn't think that would be a good idea with Arko. I didn't think the cologne scent would mix well with the cloying scent the soap came with. But while the scent isn't my cup of tea, after you rinse your face well, it's not very strong at all, especially if you wash your face afterward and use a scented after-shave balm.

The bottom line is, for a single stick, the value isn't great. It's a lot more affordable, and just as usable a product, as some of the much more expensive soaps out there, but at the single stick price, plus shipping, you could buy several pucks of Van Der Hagen locally. When you buy more sticks at a time, the per-stick price comes down, and if you can manage to get free shipping in the deal, it starts looking really attractive. The lather is very nice, the scent, not so much, but all in all, you can't go too far wrong with it. I'm dinging it one star only because of the scent. While I would rather it liquify in the microwave like VDH, that's not how the product was designed to be used, so it wouldn't be fair to dock it for that.

text:
I thought I was so great. Yes, I had read all the reviews saying these tubes dispense far too much sanitizer at once. But I was going to be careful, not like those *other* Amazon customers who don't know how to work a squeeze bottle. Well, the bottles arrived, and sure enough, they are so determined to be leak-proof that when you squeeze them, nothing happens until all of a sudden an olive-sized dollop of product comes out in one big "blurp!" Being gentle does not help; the dispensing is all or nothing. I might try to MacGyver a solution by poking a small hole somewhere to try to fix this problem, but for now these bottles are too annoying to use. The good: 1) These come in black to be less obvious when dangling from a dark bag, though this camouflage is slightly undermined by the white cap. 2) The carabiner for clipping to a purse/backpack is handy.
EDIT: Fixed the all-or-nothing dispensing problem by using scissors to make a tiny slit in one of the "petals" surrounding the tiny opening. Now a slight squeeze gives the desired small amount of product.

text: I have very long and very thick hair ... I don't always have full use of my hands due to disability so this was taking a real chance - even before my disability I've always had hair down to almost my bottom ... and at the shortest, to the edges of my shoulder blades (middle of my back) ... THIS BRUSH did NOT tangle my hair - this brush actually went through my thick hair and gave it body and allowed me to flip at the ends and where I wanted .... for me to have some height and shape - takes a lot and can be painful for me ... until I got this brush I mostly let my hair air dry and let it go wavy ... then used a curling iron .. but I like to wear my hair straight - with some "sas" .... this brush helps me in that ... it does not tear up my scalp or hurt .... it does let me use it without causing pain to my disabled hands (nerve damage from neck) ... and being able to use the brush more easily than most, I can dry my hair all the way, leaving it to look shinny and healthy and I'm so glad.  I've NEVER had a round brush that has done this .. and at such an inexpensive price ... I've spent up to $70 on hair brushes in the past .... just to make my life easier ...(not recently but way back before I was disabled) ... None of the brushes worked as well as this and I could not recommend it well enough.  I was so surprised!  Also - With a recent really really really bad hair cut ... ugh ... .this brush made it so much easier to work with my hair ...  I'm grateful to have it.

text:
I wish I had known it was so small. I have tiny hands and it fit in my hands perfectly. Was a bit smaller actually. The tattoos that were advertised for Selena’s revival promo were much bigger. But I couldn’t afford them at the time.

I also found out the hard way that if I accidentally moved the part of my body I’m doing the tattoo on just a bit, the tattoo will break apart from each other and become distorted. The G didn’t stay well on my finger. But I didn’t read the part of washing it with soap and water to help it. When I tried the Roman numerals, it finally stayed. I had to wait until about a half an hour after I applied it, keeping still. Then I very gently washed it with hand soap and lukewarm water. Then I dabbed it with a towel and put hairspray on it. It’s so far stayed for up to 8 days in a row. Fading of course, but it’s still going strong. Even with the regular shower. But I pat the tattoo with my scrubbie when I use body wash. So far that’s the only one that’s stayed. The god strengthens me and the date next to the Roman numerals failed to stay.

I’m saving the Arabic and music note because they’re my favorite. Hopefully they’ll finally stay. But unless you’ve got the time, patience and money to be able to do these, I don’t suggest buying them. Unless they’re like $2, I probably won’t be buying them again

1. No toxic ingredients
2. Much cheaper
3. It promotes healthy gums
4. It works better over time

I have developed a routine for dealing with this. I brush my teeth with sonic toothbrush and the mud right before my shower. This contouring kit is so much fun! I've been playing with contouring a little over the past few months but was trying to piece everything together with random finds at the stores and as a beginner it was hard to pick the correct colors I need to do a true contoured look. Having this palette with so many choices makes it easy for me to play and perfect my look. The powder goes on well and the colors transfer great onto skin. Using powder instead of a heavier concealer or foundation gives it a Much softer look and not like I've caked a bunch of crap onto my face. They blend together well and last all day. There are also about a billion and one videos in YouTube that show you clearly step by step. There is a good amount of powder of each color and I think this palette will last me a long time. There are many colors to fit just about every skin tone and color, a fantastic starter kit! I love it!

I did receive my contouring makeup palette free or at a promotional price for testing and evaluation purposes. text:
This stuff is amazing! I have tried extensions, false strip lashes and individual lashes and they always drive me crazy by the end of the day. Plus every time I use the glue I end up breaking and pulling out lashes. My lashes were getting short and stubby and really quite ugly. I have always had little lashes and so I thought I would at least try this stuff to see if it would help regrow and heal my lashes. It took a couple of weeks of using it religiously to see a difference but then I really started to notice.

For the first time the tips of my eyelashes were touching the skin under my brows! I was so excited! They length looked like I had false individual lashes on without mascara! The bottle did last a lot longer than it said it would so that was nice as it isn't super cheap. I put it on at night, in bed, gliding it along the line I'd put eyeliner (takes practice without a mirror ). I had very sparse lashes and are now filled in and double the length and darker. It took 3-4 months before I saw growth and now after a year, I use it on alternate nights, sometimes putting extra virgin olive oil or glycerin on them and growth is maintained. With mascara, it looks like I have extensions. At age 55, I love it. I was paying $24-28/ tube that lasted 2 months. The ONLY unfortunate thing was that some of them grew, but in a different direction so thy didn't quit curl up like the others but that is probably just my body rather than the serum. Oh, slight redness in eye if you put it on and keep eye in use (daytime use rather than sleeping) but no iris color change like is possible with LashRevive. (Rapid lash is NOT prescription but for me, works as well.) You do have to keep using it, or they will go back to the way they were. But that's fine with me because they look so nice it's worth it! I totally recommend this, just have a little patience.

text:
I've owned a few trimmers over the few years, and none had a built in vacuum. After losing the power cord to my last one, I went shopping and found this gem. The vacuum intrigued me -- would it really be worth the extra $20-25 over a standard trimmer? One word: Yes.

I'd always put off shaving my beard due to the necessity of cleaning up afterwards. I just trimmed my beard, which had grown out over the past two weeks, and amazingly, the sink below me only had a few pieces of hair in it. That is totally acceptable in my book, and with this I'll be keeping my beard trimmed often because cleanup isn't a hassle anymore.

Highly recommended. I can't speak for longevity, except that the trimmer I'm replacing was a Norelco that I bought almost two years ago, and it's still running (albeit it just died because I can't charge it anymore).

text:
I love this company's products! This is it my first purchase of temporary tattoos from this company and so I am familiar with their brands and products. I would definitely recommend their products if you're looking for high-quality tattoos that are modern and stylish.

These are super easy to put on and tends to stay for several days versus some of the other tattoos on the market. The one I have in the picture below has been on for four days now and it still looks just like the day I applied at.

I love all of the different packs of variety so that you can get a pack of that suits your style and needs. I would definitely recommend these. Application is really simple. You pick the tattoo you want and place it in side down on your skin wherever you would like it to be. Just make sure it is straight and where you want it before you apply the wet paper towel. You just take a wet paper towel and place it over the paper side of the tattoo. The other side you adhere to your skin. Then hold it there for 30 seconds, and when you left it off, the tattoo stays on your skin and the paper peels away. Then, just let it dry.

Love these! I highly recommend them. Happy shopping

text:
This is a fantastic storage and transport option for the casual makeup user. If you have a smallish makeup and brush collection, this will keep your stuff organized beautifully. I loved it for the ten months I used this. Before I had this, I'd tried makeup bags (too much digging for stuff I wanted), a single-compartment train case (also too disorganized), and Caboodles and tackle boxes (too much wasted space in the boxes, and needed too much room on the counter when open). Those storage options always left me feeling frustrated because they got in the way of putting on my makeup efficiently. This was the answer to my prayers!

I ended up having to buy a simalir organizer case in a larger size when I started ditching individual eyeshadows in favor of large palettes, and expanding my brush collection. This case held my Naked Smoky and Sugarpill Pro Palette just fine, but when I got the Modern Renaissance palette and a large Z Palette and couldn't add either of them, I downgraded this case to storing things I only need to dig out occasionally.

That said, this is a sturdy and well-built case that I had no issues with whatsoever. I used this pretty regularly, frequently taking it to/from performance gigs, and nothing ever ripped or tore, even toward the end when I had it packed to the gills. The zippers work cleanly. The strap is sturdy. The dividers stay put.

I have no reservations recommending this, unless you are a makeup junkie and/or have several large palettes. It's a great product!

why use a primer... well below are some reasons why you must:)

I loved this one because you need very little to make your face looks all smooth and fresh.

read what else I found:
If you haven’t been using makeup primer, then you’re really missing out.

Whether you wear a full face of makeup everyday or you prefer to keep it simple with BB cream and tinted lip balm, makeup primer is a godsend when trying to combat shine or create a smooth base for foundation.

Yet, many people often skip this step because they believe they don’t really need it, are unsure of how to use it or don’t think it’s worth the investment. However, using primer underneath your makeup will make everything last longer, whether that’s on your skin or in your cosmetics bag. We asked two beauty experts to discuss the ins and outs of primer and why it’s a must-have. Here’s the deal.

Makeup primer is a base for foundation or face makeup that allows it to go on smoother and last longer.

Formulas are available in cream, gel and powder. While moisturizer softens skin, editorial and celebrity makeup artist Stephen Dimmick points out that primer prepares skin for your favorite tinted moisturizer or foundation, giving it something to hold onto so it lasts throughout the day.

Many makeup primers are formulated with silicone-based polymers like dimethicone because of their ultra-smoothing effects. But for those who struggle with cystic acne and other skin conditions, do a patch test or check with a medical professional before use, as there have been reports of allergic reactions to dimethicone.

Physicians Formula’s celebrity makeup artist Joanna Schlip adds that makeup primers can also help smooth any fine lines, wrinkles or large pores. If you are searching for a face primer to help color-correct rosacea or conceal acne spots, Schlip’s go-to is this light green primer as that hue cancels out redness.

Primers are even available just for your eyelids to prevent creasing. “I’d recommend pairing a primer with extra long-wear [eyeshadow or eyeliner] formulas for a look that will really stay put,” says Schlip. If you have oilier eylids, Dimmick advises that you use a mattifying eyelid primer.

Using your fingertips to apply makeup primer allows you to really work the product into your skin.

Schlip thinks it’s best to apply primer with fingertips for areas larger than a quarter. “If only using for small areas, I’d recommend a brush. I like to use a concealer or lip brush for this,” she said. This spot-treat technique gives the look of a more natural finish, according to Schlip.

Taking this routine a step further, Dimmick considers the process “an exercise for the face.” He explains that massaging primer onto skin with fingertips helps “to get the blood flowing underneath.” We’ll consider that the next time we’re meditating while putting on makeup.

Primer is a life-saver for getting your makeup to stay-put in extreme weather.

If you live, visit or plan on traveling anywhere that experiences hot and humid or cold and dry temperatures, Schlip suggests having primer handy to help your makeup wear longer. However, Dimmick advocates for using primer year-round. “No matter what the weather is like outside, this extra step will save you a lot of time and effort.”

Primer can even be worn on its own.
Love the fresh no-makeup look but want to look a bit more polished than when you first woke up? Primer is your answer. The product can be worn on your eyelids and double as a light eyeshadow, or it can serve as a lighter alternative to foundation.

text:
Wow! I've tried a lot of different Argan Oil shampoo and conditioners (and sprays and masks and hot treatments etc) and I can 100% say this is my All time favorite for no other reason than how incredibly fast I'm seeing a difference in the ends and improvement on breakage. I color my hair and have been for close to 10 years now and just in the past year or so I've started having really bad problems with breakage and split ends. I've tried probably 10 different brands or more with some kind of argan oil treatment and it really helps. But I didn't get results like this from anywhere else, and I've tried some pretty pricey stuff. After like, 3 days of use I happened to look down at my hair one day and it just hit me that I didn't have any frizz or areas that looked wild and untamed from breakage, the hairs that would normally have 2 or 3 splits or frays were just smooth all the way down. I tried to take a picture of the hair up close so you could see but couldn't get a clear shot at such a short distance. The color looks richer and has a healthy looking shine, it isn't greasy or stringy like you might worry about with Argan Oil.
This stuff even smells amazing! I usually like coconut scents, it's always been my favorite, but this smells more floral and a little spicy, which maybe sounds weird but I think it's soooo good.

It lathers up surprisingly well for an Argan Oil shampoo and I don't have to use much for my bra strap length hair. My hair is really thick, too, but a good quarter sized dallop works out to be about the right amount for me. I do the ends and body first and work my way up to my scalp, backwards from how I use regular shampoo. (I do this because some brands seem to leave my hair oily if I don't get it rinsed out well, that Argan Oil can be stubborn.) I usually let it set for a couple minutes while I wash my body so the hair can absorb the nutrients before I rinse it away. I follow it with their conditioner and my hair gets sooo soft when it dries. I really can't stress enough how well it helps my hair. I know everyone responds differently but for me, it's better than anything else I've tried. I'll be ordering this again for sure.

I did receive my Argain oil shampoo at a promotional price for testing and evaluation purposes. I was not paid for my review and was asked only to provide whatever feedback I may have about the product, whether it be positive or negative. I welcome both positive or negative feedback as well. My review is solely based on my experience and honest opinions of this shampoo.

text:
Overall a good case for the Nintendo Switch. First off, there is no bad smell! The Switch fits great in the bottom half of the case, there are indents for the Joycon bumps and elastic straps that are found on most other cases also. The elastic straps work to keep the Switch from sliding out of the zipper is undone, but it's also difficult to put in and awkward to remove since there is no good way to pull the device up. The screen is covered by a padded and soft divider that has slots for up to eight game cartridges. The top half of the case has a loosely netted area that could easily hold cables and earbuds. If you have a smaller charger it could probably fit also. An extra set of Joycon controllers will fit, but is tight.
The exterior is not black, but more of a grey with a logo at the right top side. A bonus is the included hand strap.

text:
I'm on my third bottle of this beard oil and I'm very happy with it. I like to grow full bore mountain man beards, especially during ski season, and this stuff keeps my beard feeling soft, controls stray hairs, and even gives it a richer, fuller color than when it's dry. No joke - some random guy in the car next to me at a stop light yesterday motioned to where his beard would be, pointed at me, and gave me a thumbs up. I think Flapwings deserves some of the credit for that.

Also, why is it that 90% of my beard compliments are from other guys? Can't blame Flapwings for that but it’s a thing. I've had at least three people yell "nice beard!" or some variation of that while driving past. Am I being cat called? Is this sexual harassment? I don't know the answers but if you want a beard that compels strangers to yell compliments at you then give this stuff a try.

text:
This is an excellent assortment of attractive, quality gel polish, with base coat and top coat (no wipe and matte versions). The presentation elevates the worth as a gifting option. One of the best features of this set is the bottles of gel polish provide actual representations of the contents. On the top of the cap is a sticker with proper identification of the color (including glitter, confetti varieties). Also on the front label is a small circle, under the brand name, that has an accurate portrayal of the contents. Finally, a company gets it. So, this boosts the rating a bit.
There are 16 varieties of gel polish, with confetti and glitter. The consistency is perfect, allowing application of a thin coat. An ability to apply a thin coat is essential, mainly due to the size of these bottles. They are 5ml (0.17fl oz), which is one of the smallest bottles of gel polish that i've encountered. This is actually a disappointing size/quantity of gel polish. Based on the size of the bottles of gel polish, I consider this to actually be a sampler set, as opposed to a set of 'normal' sized bottles of attractive confetti, glitter gel polishes. So, i guess one can try out the various polishes and then purchase larger bottles of desired varieties.
Overall, I feel all of the polishes in the assortment are visually attractive. The polishes are excellent quality, and produce excellent results. The significant other tested the gel on some fake nails, and i commented that it looks beautiful as is...no need to add any embellishments/decorations. She agreed., but said she'd combinde solid color gel polish with these, and add some rhinestones or other decorations, maybe create a marbled effect. I could see her creative juices flowing.
The product does match the descriptions and pictures in the product details. The presentation is effective and top notch. We like the quality and consistency of the gel polishes and base/top coats. If the bottles were at least the size of the base/top coat bottles, this set would be perfect and worth 5 stars. As currently configured, based on observations and impressions, this quality 'sampler' set earns a 4.5 - 4.75 star rating, with a high level recommendation.

text: I'm new to nail polish and this was the first time I EVER did my nails with gel polish. I have had professional applications but never do I paint my own nails, like on a rare occasion I'd use clear polish or a light glitter so believe me I'm brand new to all this nail stuff. This powder is AMAZING and so beautiful. I found it helpful to cure the top coat for half the time, apply the powder more saturated at the nail tip and rub upward to the cuticle with the applicator tip to help buff the powder. A little powder goes a long way and next time I will apply MUCH less powder, too much powder gives a metallic look. I had a more sheer halo look which Ioved but I messed up the other hand and had apply more powder to even the look out lol. My nails didn't come out perfect because I'm very inexperienced in nail care but I'm really happy with the results and will continue to practice. I highly recommend this powder. I used gellen gel polish romantic color set, gellen base and top coat and the sunuv 48 watt smart lamp 2.0 and this powder.  I had sucess with all these products. I was hesitant to try the powder after spend so much time with the gel polish but I'm really glad I played with it. I'll will definitely order this again. Just try it, it's fun. The only down side is that you will become obsessed looking at your nails. I got outside specifically to see the pretty halo twinkle at me in the bright sun light haha 🤩

text:
613-20”-bangs

I would buy this again! It came in a bag, tangle free and had a slight smell to it. Bangs are long enough to be cut to desired length. In both pictures they are uncut.

The first picture is it straight out of the bag, only styling the bangs with a flat iron. The second picture is after a color bath and styling.

THE ENDS TAKE COLOR QUICKLY! I dipped mine in a purple shampoo bath, leaving the ends out (only allowing the water to touch it when squeezing the water out) and they still took more color than the top of the wig, along with the bangs. Holds styling well, went all day without losing curls even in the snow.

I would say it’s all human hair, I don’t see any plastic hairs or had any melting when styling.

The wig is 20” from top to bottom, though I was expecting the hair to be 20”. It’s a comfortable stretchy mesh cap, and fits my larger head snuggly. My head is about 23” around, so heads 22”-24” should be fine wearing this.

text: I appreciate a good facial cream, as I used to use regular mens body cream to moisturize my face.  That worked ok, but I found that it would sometimes leave me feeling a little greasy.  This cream is thick yet not too greasy at all.  It does leave my face feeling very nicely moisturized, and never dry. The quantity doesn't seem like a lot, but for this cream, a little goes a long way.  So I doubt I'll run out anytime soon.  I also appreciate that it doesn't really have a scent, though a nice scent sometimes isn't all bad.  I just can't stand creams that are over perfumed, and to be honest, it doesn't seem like a great idea to me to add scent to something that goes on the sensitive skin of ones face, so I appreciate that this one does not.  All in all, I have been using it for a few weeks, and have had good moisturizing results, which does well to reduce wrinkles in the long run.  I find that moisturizing is a fine line because too much can cause acne, not enough can cause acne, but this one plays in the Goldilocks zone of just right.  I'll certainly keep using it, and do recommend it.

text:
I know this is a baby bath oil, but I'm a fan of using bath oils as shave oils. A little goes a long way, just a few drops per leg (or you can add a drop to your regular shave gel). Helps reduce irritation and you can see where you are shaving! I can't live without it. It's better than any foam, gel or lotion on the market. Truly the best shave experience. I have thick hair and dry flaky skin that is prone to itching and sensitivity. I use this bath oil with a cheapie disposable razor and a few drops of body oil after a shower and my legs are silky for days. Literally days. No razor burn, no irritation, no frustration. I prefer unscented products and this is truly unscented--thank you! Please use caution when using oils in the tub, but I haven't had any problems with the tub getting slippery. Only thing I would change in a better bottle with a pump or dropper top. It's easy to use too much. A little goes a long way so if you're hesitant about the price, don't be, because you'll end up spending more in the long run on something cheaper.

Also, used this as a body wash after getting a little too much sun today, and my skin was super soothed. I think it will help prevent my skin from developing a sunburn--bonus! I may just have to buy a vat of this!

Hope you found this review helpful!

text:
This is an interesting way to get some very nice curls in your hair. It’s a long soft rod type thing. The material is the usual you’d get for a curler wrapped in silk. It’s all bendy and what you do is sort of braid your hair onto/with this. After that, you use the included scrunchies to hold it in place at the bottom.

This sounds all well and good but, maybe it was just me, it was difficult to get my hair twisted correctly onto the rod. My hair is long, reaching about mid back and perfectly straight. If you get it on there right, it comes out looking very nice. It just takes a whole lot of practice. Also, as a side note, if your hair is a little damp, it works better.

This comes with the main curler, two large scrunchies, two smaller scrunchies, two hair clips, and a nice bag to keep everything in. The scrunchies are silk.

This works nicely and it’s worth trying out if you want heatless curls. For me it was difficult to get the knack of but if you are good with hair, which I am just not, you might find it quite easy from the start.

text: I ordered the Dewy Bloom in the color #105 Ardour（Terracotta). This is a pretty shade of a light mauve and works well with my skin tone. To use, you must remove the cap, then twist the top to the "on" position. Gently squeeze until you move the product up into the sponge tip applicator. I like the applicator because I can dot my cheeks, then use my blender brush to blend it into my makeup. I have found that I do need to build it up to my desired pigment, but that is easy to do. Once I am done with it, I simply twist the top into the "off" position and replace the cap. I've found that the color does last all day, but I do apply it on top of a foundation and use setting spray as well. Overall, I think this liquid blush does an ok job, but you do have to build it up as it's quite sheer.

text:
Candies was one of my all time favorites and I hated that it was discontinued. So you can imagine my excitement when i found out it was back on the market. I was so excited in fact that i blind purchased 3 bottles off the bat cause i thought “who knows how long its back for!?”

Well….unfortunately this relaunch does not deserve the name. This new juice is a completely different recipe. Does it smell good? I mean…..ehh…very generic fresh scent like one you’d get from an Axe or Bod body spray. It does not smell like Candies and its nothing great or even good. I would say its just “ok.”

Also it doesn't last very long at all. I know body chemistry is different on everyone but i typically hold on to scents all day and this one was completely gone without a trace after about 1.5 hrs.

If you are looking for the OG candies, THIS IS NOT IT. I am in my late 30s and will probably give the bottles to my sons who are under 13. Pre teens and teens is who i see wearing this.

text:
I have fine hair so i'm always looking for the best products that will a) not damage my hair and b) make it look good for the full day. These little hairbands do both! I have always used scrunchies to pull together outfits, but my hair stylist told me that, due to my fine hair, I really shouldn't be wearing scrunchies since they tend to pull at the hair too much and can be damaging. She recommended me these hairbands and my hair has been way healthier ever since I switched. (though, I will admit that I add a scrunchy around these hairbands every now and again, but with the hairbands doing the bulk of the work, the scrunchy just adds some flare it's a nice little secret and way for me to have it all lol)

Also these are such a cute color so i like keeping a couple around my wrist. Fun to play with for those who are anxious like me, too lol.

And I like that it doesn't leave a crease in my hair, too. My hair, since it's so fine, tends to be ruined anytime i put it in an updo since it makes that weird bump/ crease lol. so i can wear my hair in an updo with these babies during the day and then still put it down at night without having to worry about a straightener to hide any bumps!

definitely a good buy for anyone looking to have a healthier updo!!

text:
I love these!
I had read about doing lotion masks using cotton pads that peeled apart into layers, and bought the stuff to try it. It's good, and not hard to do, but takes a bit of time to prepare. So I bought these compressed sheet masks for doing quick lotion masks. I really like these. The compressed size makes it easy to travel with them, and they don't take up hardly any space when you store them. When you get them wet, they expand like the snake pellets you get on 4th of July. =)
I keep a shot glass in my bathroom just for doing quick lotion masks. I unwrap one of these compressed masks, toss it in the shot glass, then douse it with skin conditioner, hyaluronic acid lotion, and a bit of rose water. Once the mask is nice and saturated, I put it on my face for 3-5 minutes. (Since I like to listen to music while I'm getting ready, I leave the mask on for about the length of a song.)
The cotton material feels nice, not scratchy. When the sheet is wet it has enough stretch to cling to the face like it should. The fit is wide, the the nose flap is short. At least that's the fit on my face. I fix this by carefully cutting the excess width at my temples with scissors and placing one of strips I've cut over the end of my nose where the nose flap doesn't cover. The main thing I like about these is that the flaps on the eye holes are attached at the top. So, while I'm wearing the sheet mask, I can put the eye flaps on my eye lids so they get some moisture and still see what I'm doing. I really like that.
I plan on repurchasing these as soon as I run out.

Similar to some of the other reviewers experiences, I find that the SnozzlePro works pretty well for me, but I do have to be very careful and make frequent adjustments -- it sort of exploits design problems with the tools you are using. Pros:
-It definitely fits my Conair Infinity Pro and probably won't have any trouble fitting other hair dryers.
-It's thick enough to be sturdy, but thin enough that I can stretch it onto the hair dryer.
- I don't experience any leaking or weird smells.
-I can use it (carefully) with the Xtava.

Cons:
-It is made of rubber, so when it heats up the rubber becomes more pliable and loose around the diffuser. I would recommend using this with cold air if possible. Unfortunately, my Conair Infinity Pro has a setting for "Cool" that isn't cold enough. So, I find that this works best if I keep my finger on the blue "Cold Shot" button the whole time.
-The Xtava has a very short connection point, so even though the SnozzlePro works pretty well, there's not a ton of space for it to hold on. That's a problem with the Xtava though, not the SnozzlePro. In particular, it seems to detach most at the top of the connection point and it will fall off while I'm using it. I just sort of use it gently and keep an eye on that top part to make sure it isn't slipping out.

Overall, I think the SnozzlePro is a great product that would probably work best with a diffuser with a longer connection point than the Xtava and potentially a hair dryer that has a truly cold setting.

text:
This Aloe Vera Gel by Sdara Skincare takes a few minutes to fully absorb into my skin. I worried it would leave behind a sticky feeling, since I could feel it on my arm the whole time it took to dry. But once the gel finally absorbed, I couldn’t feel any residue left behind. I didn’t notice much of any scent either, which is another positive for me.

The gel is clear; the plastic bottle is amber colored to help keep light out. I wish the bottle had a flip top or squeeze lid instead of a screw off cap.

The list of ingredients is what gives me pause. I’m not sure why sodium bicarbonate (baking soda) would need to be added to the organic aloe leaf juice. The product description mentions “we perfected our serum by enriching with Vitamin E, & Citric Acid for improved nutrient absorption.” I don’t see Vitamin E listed in the ingredients.

I will probably end up using this aloe vera gel to make hand sanitizer. The gel is thick, so it should be good for that use.
