[[VIDEOID:a4a584a64c290ec35e3eb063c37e5b8d]] I recently was able to purchase the LuxorWare Foot Spa Massager sold by Luxor Outlet in a gorgeous coffee/cream color scheme and let me tell you, it is amazing. It is so easy to use ~ it basically comes out of the box ready to use...All I had to do was pop on the wheels & add water and it was ready to go. I was worried it would be difficult to use but I can honestly say that it is beyond user friendly...each function has a separate button making it extremely easy to customize for the perfect foot massage.
So let me give you the details ~
It comes with motorized roller covers (you actually get two styles of interchangeable massage plates) that hit acupressure points in your feet, water circulation and heat (adjustable) therapy, air bubbles and water jets to stimulate foot reflex zone, an Aqua Massage from calf to toe) and a super easy to use splash proof touch panel with LED screen display
You can also adjust the temp and set different timers and add essential oils. This is the most complete home foot spa I have ever seen. I have used it probably every other night since I purchased ~ my feet have never felt so pampered!!

text:
Fantastic charger - hits the QC 2.0 amperage guidelines, and will charge a Moto X to nearly full from 20% in about 30-40 minutes. Be sure you're using a cable that will support it, and just check that your phone gives you the "Turbo Power Connected" pop-up. Below is an older review - my first one appeared to be defective, Anker reached out to me and shipped a replacement. 5 star product, 5 star customer service.

When it works, this is an excellent charger. The one gripe I have with it is that it seems to need to be unplugged and plugged back into the wall for the quick charge to kick in (tested with different cables). The non-QC ports charge quickly as well, and it has the typical Anker high quality feel to it.

text: The Dry Look, at last look, was selling (if one can still find it as the quantities dwindle) for many times its old retail price. In this case, it's just another fine product now in the past (at least at this writing, one can only hope).  PLEASE NOTE After a long search I found SUAVE non-aerosol, unscented to be equal to The Dry Look but you pay less than $3.00 for 12 oz instead of from $110 to #150 for the Dry Look. Indeed, for a while we thought then name was slightly misspelled because the product is somewhat fancy. It's clearly a good quality sunscreen and experience has shown us that the odds for a Korean cosmetics product to be good are quite... good.

text:
Buyer beware!
Pros:
Packaging was great. Nothing spilled out or burst open. Colors are gorgeous.

Shipping was ok. I got a message basically saying shipping was delayed due to my error of not providing the right address, yet I ordered 4 items the same day and provided the same shipping address. They supposedly corrected the error and got it shipped to me in less than a week.

Now, let's talk about the cons:
I can't say this product is a true polygel. When using the slip solution or isopropyl alcohol, the solution dissipated and the brush dried out within seconds. Once I was able to start smoothing the product out, I got one good pat before the product became extremely sticky and unmanageable due to the brush drying out. It definitely took some finesse to get it to work. Also, do not use the dual forms as the product is not thick enough to hold it's own without moving under the light. If you have paper forms or nail tips, you will have much better luck with this product. I will say it definitely took me way too long to do one set but filing the product was great and so easy.

After trial and error, if you want to take the time to make it thicker, put some on a smooth flat surface and add clear acrylic. Make sure to use a metal spatula to mix the 2 products if you so choose to. Also, THIS PRODUCT IS VERY STICKY. If you are not careful, you will get this product everywhere.

I would give more stars if the polygel was a thicker constancy and the brush didn't dry out seconds after dipping into the slip solution or isopropyl alcohol.

I will give this kit a try again when it's time for me to change my set, but I'm not sure I will purchase this kit again.

text:
Wow! Where has this been all of my life?

I purchased this because I was intrigued by the color changing aspect. I didn't want anything that felt like a heavy mask, but wanted something that offered some coverage. This is a home run!

I'm 51 years of age. As a teen I spent a lot of time outdoors, and I do mean a LOT. Without any type of sunblock, working long days on the farm, and just as many dousing myself with baby oil and water to "get color". Now my complexion has changed, partly due to years of sun damage and more so due to the fact that I'm no longer in my youth. My skin is dry & I have a lot of fine lines and red, blotchiness. I fall in a medium skin tone.

My teen daughter has beautiful fair skin that is prone to acne. She's only started wearing minimal makeup very recently, and didn't want anything heavy or difficult to wear.

I thought this the perfect opportunity to really test this product. Two females, different ages, different skin tones. How would it measure up?

Superbly! I didn't not feel that I was wearing a heavy mask. I cannot tell you the number of liquid foundations I have tried in the 35 or so years since I started wearing makeup, but it is a LOT, with disappointing results. Hours spent blending, blending, blending....trying to get those heavy foundations blended so as not to appear as though I was wearing a mask, with the lines around the jaw area, ears, etc.

With this I did not have to do that. It goes on lightly, blends effortlessly, and held up well all day until I took it off. I'm a gal who sweats. There's no pretty way of saying it. I don't glisten. I'm at that age where hot flashes are the norm; add to that that I live in the armpit of the South where humidity is 80% or so most days and the simplest of activities like loading/unloading groceries from the car results in a deluge of sweat!

I didn't take it easy when I wore this. Believe me, it was tested. But at no time did I have to reapply, or feel like I'd lost any of it as I went about my day. It was truly THAT good.

My daughter is equally thrilled with it. Did it disguise her few blemishes? No, but it did cover the redness of them and makes her look more "put together".

I'm placing my order for a second bottle of it as soon as I post this, so that each of us will have our own.

text: This is an excellent product.  I've been using this for almost a year now, and will continue to buy it.  I have read the other two reviews, and my experience with it is far more favorable.  I was looking for a fluid, non oily sunscreen to use under my mineral makeup or under my Laura Mercier tinted moisturizer.  This turned out to be perfect.  It has a milky consistency (not oily) and absorbs almost immediately and leaves a smooth finish (not sticky or oily).  It does have a brightening effect after application.  Any kind of makeup can be applied over it.  It has SPF 40, which is very difficult to find. And the sunscreen lasts all day long.  I never ever burn after applying this, even when I've been outside in the sun for hours.  I will agree that the pump doesn't work great (hence the 4 stars).  I've learned to just press the pump lightly into the palm of my hand, to reduce the odds of it spraying or streaming out irregularly.  But that doesn't keep me from wanting to continue using it.  One small bottle also lasts a long time (like 6 months or more)... because a little goes a long way.

text:
Doesn't matter where you buy them, who you buy from, etc. There is a 50% chance you get a good bottle of polish and a 50% chance you get a bad one. I have 3 bottles from 3 different places, 3 different colors (blue white and black) and the blue one is good, the white one is meh, and the black one is completely useless. The white and black have bent brushes, and the white polish is a bit dried out but not the worst, kind of reminds me of using white-out before they changed the formula and it got all watery and they changed to a sponge applicator from a brush. The black one is awful - it's literally just clumps of dried out polish with some occasional damp spots. The blue one is okay, the polish is alright, but I wouldn't recommend using the brushes that came with these but buying a separate skinny brush for line work or whatever it is you want to do.

I should mention I have been using them to fix an enameled piece of jewelry as opposed to doing nails, as that's what the jeweler recommended (nobody local repairs enamel resin and buying enamel is very expensive and you end up with a ton leftover), so a jeweler I trust recommended nail polish with a good resin sealant and that's what I've been doing. Initially I had used an X-Acto knife to cut painter's tape around the shapes of different colors and metal but switched to this pink-colored liquid latex paint I saw on a youtube video for nail designs and it's much easier.

text:
Notes out of the box;
* Very nice, low profile footprint design.
* 7 inches long, 5 inches at widest part of the oval, 2.5 inches tall.
* Fits up to .75 oz polishes with all shaped bottle necks (bam! take that iZen Pro!)
* Silver plastic, no idea on solvent resistance (yet).
* Uses both AC adapter or batteries for cordless operation.
* 5600 shakes per minute promises the box and spec detail.
* 3 detachable, adjustable rubber securing straps.
* 1 on/off button, with a lighter pressure 'pulse on' feature.

Operation:
* Cinch to strap on.
* Pretty impressive agitation for one so wee.
* Noise level mild especially in comparison to my incessant bottle agitation whining. "Ow! My arms! Blah, blah, blah." I'd be more specific, but I was operating it right on a stainless steel counter to put it through the noise test. It'd be unladylike to compare it to a 'personal massager' out loud wouldn't it? Did I just type that? Oopsy, my bad. Quieter over a mat, silicone, towel or cork tile by miles. I did that too.

Result:
Before had separation, some streaky clearness and clinging pigment near the neck. I gave it my regular human powered roll/tap/roll for the following 'before shaker' application demo. (See posted pics)

Left, regular human agitation. 5 freaky coats and I had to wrangle dual directions and mucho smoothing in application. I tried really hard to give it more care actually because I wanted to be ungenerous to ForPro. But, human agitation was a streaky, bubbled shrink-back, difficult, wrinkling mess at every application and cure. Just like last time I dared to be decadent. I purposefully used a problem child color of an otherwise fabulous gel polish line. Plus, I left the cleansing paper towel wipes in the photos to demonstrate the horrid vs acceptable cure difference. Like eww! That's dangerous. It even wiped off in spots!

Right, ForPro shaken. 3 coats. Application was easy and like normally behaved other vampy, dark, well agitated gel polishes. Say what? I thought I'd see some improvement, but I didn't really expect this great of one. The color is wearable and I like it now.

All things are equal in my test, except I was more careful in application of the human shaken swatch, which has 2 more coats for a subpar, unusable result. I had to, it was having a tantrum in streaky pants before ForPro.

Other notes:
ForPro requires a phillips head screwdriver to open the 4 AA battery housing. Probably because it shakes so much? Still, a nice cordless option to the AC adapter also included. Yay that. *Batteries not included.

And, I think any warranty would be online via ForPro website only? This didn't come with instructions or warranty card in the box. Mostly a no-brainer plug and play.

It's unscathed by alcohol meaning any gel polish mishaps can wipe right off. I blotted the underbelly to remove dust lightly.

Clear rubber feet stay put while leaving no marks. That's awesome. Especially compared to Robarts that jiggle off tables and sound like landscaping machines.

I like ForPro so far. I think it saves product and my shake stress physiology. Prettier result in fewer, easier coats :).

It did a good job on both a chunky glitter and a streak-prone frost in a conventional lacquer brand. I did shake them once to break surface tension first though. I also rotated the bottles and let them shake twice. The beads were rolling free in both and additive distribution was even and good after. Thanks ForPro! Please last an eon now.

text:
First off, let me say that I did not buy it here at Amazon, but locally here in singapore.

Exceptional, is what i would call it. I'm certainly impressed.

To-date, i've used Gillette Platinums (blue,white box), Gillette 7 o'clock Super Platinums (black packs), Gillette 7 o'clock Super Stainless, Voskhod, Astra SP, Concord, Croma, amongst a few others.

I would place the Gillette 7 o'clock Permasharps at the same top tier with Gillette 7 o'clock Super Platinum (indian), and the Gillette Platinum (russian).

But any blade, like a woman, needs the right approach to bring out her/its best. Hence, i'll describe my shaving regimen. (a better regimen might bring out even more from this blade perhaps.)

1. I always have a hot shower before shaving, thus bringing out the hairs more.
2. I use the Rex Ambassador safety razor at a (extremely mild) setting of 1, thus contributing to the smoothness of the shave.
3. I use a combination of shower soap (bar) with sunflower oil (cooking), hand-lathered till the lather is thin but dense, not watery. No brushes, shaving creams, shaving soap, scuttles, etc....completely unnecessary.
4. I only go with-the-grain except for my neck. (which would certainly contribute to the longevity of the blade).

My stubble is hard and wiry and i do daily face and head-shaves. I also do a once-weekly body shave, and a daily front shave from chest to netherregions.

After the shave, i pat-dry the blade with a microfibre cloth, and then strop it against the cloth on all 4 sides a couple of times each.

Ranking
_______

Sharpness -
1. G 7oc Sp
2. G 7oc Permasharp tied with Gillette Platinum

Smoothness -
1. G Permasharp (gets smoother on 2nd and 3rd day) tied with G Platinum
2. G Super Platinum

Longevity (for both head and face-shaves in terms of days) -
1. G 7oc Permasharp - 8 and counting
2. G 7oc SP - 8
3. G platinum - 6-7

I judge the blade's longevity in terms of the degree to which i can get a clean shave with a single swipe. I always do a 2nd swipe just to get any tiny stubble that might be left behind, but the first swipe must perform at 95%. So when i say that a blade lasts for 6 days, i mean that i get 95% only after 2 swipes and not 1.

So, in sum, i definitely recommend this blade. I just picked up 300 yesterday, and will pick up more up to a thousand the next time i go to the shop where i get it from here in singapore. (cost : $9.00 sgd per 100 blades here.)

3. i use a combination of shower soap (bar) with sunflower oil (cooking), hand-lathered till the lather is thin but dense, not watery. no brushes, shaving creams, shaving soap, scuttles, etc....completely unnecessary.
4. i only go with-the-grain except for my neck. (which would certainly contribute to the longevity of the blade).

my stubble is hard and wiry and i do daily face and head-shaves. i also do a once-weekly body shave, and a daily front shave from chest to netherregions.

after the shave, i pat-dry the blade with a microfibre cloth, and then strop it against the cloth on all 4 sides a couple of times each.

ranking
_______

sharpness -
1. g 7oc sp
2. g 7oc permasharp tied with gillette platinum

smoothness -
1. g permasharp (gets smoother on 2nd and 3rd day) tied with g platinum
2. g super platinum

longevity (for both head and face-shaves in terms of days) -
1. g 7oc permasharp - 8 and counting
2. g 7oc sp - 8
3. g platinum - 6-7

i judge the blade's longevity in terms of the degree to which i can get a clean shave with a single swipe. i always do a 2nd swipe just to get any tiny stubble that might be left behind, but the first swipe must perform at 95%. so when i say that a blade lasts for 6 days, i mean that i get 95% only after 2 swipes and not 1.

so, in sum, i definitely recommend this blade. i just picked up 300 yesterday, and will pick up more up to a thousand the next time i go to the shop where i get it from here in singapore. (cost : $9.00 sgd per 100 blades here.)

text:
I bought my first box years ago (when I seem to remember it being the old packaging being labeled as brow safe?). I just finished up another box (3rd? 4th?) and noticed that it’s not meant to be used on the brows. It’s not difficult to keep far away from your eyes, just exercise caution.

I used to apply it for the recommended couple of minutes and got decent results, but now that I’m used to the performance I’ve left it on for as long as 40 minutes and have had the same color payoff but much longer lasting color. I have clear blonde brows despite being a natural dark brunette, so anything I can do to prevent fading helps.

Recommendations:
- Block/outline your brows with a white eyeliner before applying so you can just fill in the shape without having to be too precise with the actual dye- this also keeps skin staining within the area of the brow and less noticeable
- Dye at night or when you can stay in for a couple of days if you’re worried about skin staining
- Dont exfoliate the day of dyeing and the skin stain will come up easier
- use a cotton bud lightly dipped in skin salve/Vaseline to scrub up skin stain rather than washing it away to avoid early fading on brow hairs- even just applying a bit to brows and leaving it on for a while before wiping it away can really get it off much easier AND avoid irritating the skin
- wipe away areas you want to be lighter half way through your dyeing process (Ex: if you want the inner part of the brow to be lighter than the tail, wipe away some dye on the inner part and leave more to develop on the tail)
- You can reapply dye if you feel it didn’t tint enough in one session. Just leave any you didn’t use up on the counter and only discard extra after you’re satisfied with the color payoff (don’t save dye for another day, just go ahead and reapply right after taking off the first application if you feel you want more color)

text:
I received these within two days of ordering. The were both packaged just fine no issues at all with that. The individual pots are on a handy case which holds all of them. They are numbered on the bottom of each pot, which corresponds with the card included, which has instructions on one side and pictures of each powder on a nail on the other, with numbers. I used 5 different powders this evening, playing with all my new stuff. Four of them were double sealed, all had tape around the pot, but the holographic one did not have an inner seal. No significant powder in the case.

Method used: prepped nails for gel normally with base, cured, 2 coats of black cured after each, one coat of no wipe top coat, cured, rubbed in the powder with the brush provided, another no wipe to coat, cure, then regular top coat and cured. They are gorgeous.

I used the silver on two nails, red on one, green on one, blue on two, and holo on two. I used another company's product on the other three. This is the best holo powder I've used so far. It shows the effect constantly. The other ones went on easily and I am very pleased with my results, they definitely are chrome enough that if I go get a pedicure with them on, I can guarantee I will be showing the employees this product on Amazon. They will ask, call each other over, and ask again and again. So far every time I go in with a chrome or holo powder on my fingernails, this happens. I'm not a nail pro, I just do them for myself, and I could not be more pleased

So, I didn't use it so I cannot comment on this product. However, I think the company should have been more careful about this "product switch" so we don't have to go through the trouble of returning a product that is different than previously sold.

text: According to the product label, the purpose of the Silver Miracles Colloidal Silver Foot Cream is to hydrate, lubricate, moisturize, and soothe dry skin, AND kill odor-causing bacteria. From what I can tell, it seems to do this, but not in a dramatic fashion. The ingredients include Almond Oil, Cocoa Seed Butter, Shea Butter, Coconut Oil, Urea, Peppermint Oil, Menthol, and Colloidal Silver. As others have pointed out, the cream spreads easy enough but doesn't seem to absorb that well into the skin. Also, the dinky-sized jar only contains 2-ounces. So, you'll probably go through the bottle fairly quickly if you follow the instructions and apply liberally. It's a decent foot cream overall but seems a little pricey for the small amount you get.

text:
Very lovely shades of berry, violet, and purples exactly what I was looking for in a purple palette, lots of deep matte shades as well as shimmers. At least one duochrome shade (possibly two but I really can't tell). However as lovely as this all is, it was packaged poorly with heavier items in the box and no bubble wrap. Most of the shadows were utterly shattered. I had attempted to press them back in, but this did not work. Sadly the palate is unusable.

I would recommend if you do order this, make sure there's nothing remotely heavy in the same Amazon order to be on the safe side. The shadow formula seems to be more fragile (more soft) than most other Colourpop palettes I have purchased in the past, so this likely contributes to this as well.

text: First of all I would've bought this even if I hated the color story &thought it was trash, just to support Anwen! If you're unaware of her, she's an affordable makeup reviewer on YouTube &she's an absolute doll! The woman is absolutely delightful &although long winded, very charming. She professes to not be any good at makeup (LIES) &one of her catch phrases is "I mean really guys!" hence the front of the sleeve. All of the shade names are names she picked out for people &places that are near &dear to her. Like her husband "hunies' &her two boys &father (pamps). She's one of the most down to earth, humble, funny youtubers I watch &she deserves all of our support. That being said, the palette itself is phenomenal! I'm no beginner at makeup &I find this to be a very good mix of color. It's actually a rainbow palette with a few pops of neutral &a black with blue microglitters. Upon arm swatching the palette, I found the black to be sort of sheer, the lighter yellowy green (alex) to be sheer &the most beautiful shade in the palette (icy queen) to be hard to swatch but only because it's 100% a topper shade. It's one of the most gorgeous mutlichrome shades I own! It looks light pink in the pan but it's actually an iridescent blue/lilac w/a pink shift. Stunning. It transforms literally any shade into something spectacular &can also make a gorgeous face highlight if you love colorful highlights like me. For the pricepoint? Worth it all day long even just for that one shade!! So happy I got it!

text:
I'll start by saying the shipping was impressively fast compared to the expectation that was set, so that was a nice suprise! But then I opened up my USPS box to find 4 packages of 'China Glaze' branded boxes with the Essie polish inside. I won't dwell on that too much though because they were safely packaged and all arrived in perfect condition. I just felt like the inconsistent branding was a little curious and makes me question the seller.

Ok from here I was off to a descent start color wise when I saw the first one was a nice shade of pink. Then there was another pink.. and yet another. Each only varying ever so slightly, not enough to warrant all 3 in one shipment. Next I find a bright orange, black, navy blue, gold sparkle, bright blue, another blue, and wine sparkle. This is clearly a lot of polishes no one wants and is really a "you get what you pay for" situation. I am very disappointed at this because most reviews said that was not the case. What a bummer... stuck with a lot of ugly polishes I will never use.. except maybe 1? In summary, I pretty much just paid $30ish for Qty.1 Essie polish. In my opinion this is a waste of money because I could've had 3 polishes I love for the same price instead of 10 I'll never use.

text: I was intrigued by these spiral curlers when I saw them. I've used foam rollers occasionally in the past with fair results, so I thought these might work as well. These do curl, but they aren't great. First of all, these take some practice to get in. Take small strands of hair, or you will cause tangling. I recommend doing this after a shower. text:
Shaving has always been a difficult task for me. I have extremely thick black hair that grows back almost as soon as I shaved it and would take me FOREVER to do it!! I am a busy single mom with an autistic child leaving me little to no time to shave but NOT ANYMORE!!!

I LOVE the fact I can use this wet or dry! This was a game changer for me! For all those times your in a hurry, you have pants on, but its blazing hot outside! You cant put shorts on because you haven't shaved! Well not anymore ladies! I can now throw shorts on and run this bad boy real quick and BAM!! Hair free in no time!!

Oh and if your wondering, yes I have tried using it on my underarms, bikini line, and other areas. I'm happy to report it works great all over! Now it's not as close of a shave as a razor but it does an amazing job for an electric razor! If you charge it for the required amount of time before using it the battery will stay strong and last long enough to shave EVERYTHING!

The only struggle I've had with it is removing the top part for cleaning. I'm always afraid I'm going to break it so make sure you follow the directions on how to do that! I'm so happy I made this purchase! I hope this has help you decide to make life easier & purchase one too!! Thanks for reading! :)

It should be noted that the two active ingredients are just Wolfsbane (amica montana) and common table salt (natrum muriaticum). Salt is the antibacterial substance, but wolfsbane.. isn't proven to actually do anything great. Also, be sure to avoid cuts and scrapes:

"The US Food and Drug Administration has classified Arnica montana as an unsafe herb because of its toxicity. It should not be taken orally or applied to broken skin where absorption can occur" (quote from Wikipedia, but you can look it up yourself. There are other sources that say the same thing)

.. Doesn't mean I won't buy em. I like the tea tree oil and all that. Just know you're paying kind of a lot for salt-soaked napkins.

please consider making one of the eye brushes an eyeliner brush, going forward.. i am using the smallest eye brush w/the firmest bristles to be a makeshift liner and.. only slightly successful.. five stars, tho, packed well and i could see them lasting at least a year.

thank you,

text:
In an effort to stretch the amount of time in between haircuts for my husband, I've begun giving him little beard trims here and there to straighten it out or shape it up. I'll also trim some of the curls around/above his ears and at the neckline so that it's less messy for work. I don't do anything drastic by any means, but I have a steady hand for that sort of cut.

Before, I was using whatever scissors I had around (I know, that's blasphemy in some circles). The most recent were KitchenAid kitchen shears (that I don't use for food items because I like my other kitchen scissors better), believe it or not. They are pretty sharp, so it's not quite as bad as it may sound.

At any rate, I knew that made for a sad hair trim, so I decided to upgrade. This kit happened to also have a beard comb, so that was just a bonus.

Oh man... I've been impressed across the board here. When I opened the Amazon box, there was a very nice display box by the manufacturer, with a magnetic lid and an opening made perfectly for the items. It makes for a great first impression, so this would be awesome as a gift.

You can tell just by looking that the scissors are nice, but when you pick them up, it's like the metal is buttery and soft to the touch. It feels classy, if that makes any sense. And while I've barely used them so far (I trimmed him up super recently), let me tell you... it's like night and day. It's such a smooth cut. This is how it's meant to feel when you cut hair - I just know it. I've never looked forward to giving a hair trim so much!

He already had a wooden beard comb, so I personally wasn't all that enthused about it like I was the scissors, but I have since been informed by him that the teeth are smaller on this one than on the other, which makes for a different beard combing experience. Now he has options, which he appreciates.

Oh, and the case is nice as well. :) Seriously, you'll feel good about giving this as a gift. From the moment they unwrap the box, they'll be pleased with what they see.

Please note, I received this item at a discount in exchange for my honest, unbiased opinion. I wouldn't steer anyone wrong, so if you have found this review to be helpful, please click the Yes box below. Thanks!

text:
I love Plant Therapy as a company for their pure, often organic, plant based ingredients.

The company that produces my favorite shea butter blended body butter decided not to produce it anymore. Or if they are, I can't find it anywhere. I scoured the internet looking for a replacement, read lots of reviews, did my research.

I decided on Plant Therapy's unscented body butter because I liked the idea of using my own essential oils to scent it if I wanted...or not!

The consistency is just okay. It's not particularly thick or rich. It moisturizes "okay". I wouldn't say it's any different or better than some hand lotions I have.

The unscented part is perfect. Don't detect a thing.

What bugs me the most (and if this part weren't happening, I would have scored this product much higher) is that it beads up after I massage it in. At first I thought I had really dry skin and it was flaking off as I rubbed it in. Nope. It's the cream. I have to use a towel to wipe off the annoying little beads that roll up and stick all over my hands.
It's annoying, and makes me not want to reach for it.

Sorry, Plant Therapy, I wish I loved this product more!

text: I am new to using the hair straightening brush, and I LOVE IT! I have naturally curly hair, and most of the time, I just want to smooth out my curls , and not necessarily straighten them. Before getting the straightening brush, it took me a good 15-20 minutes using a large barrel curling iron to soften my curls, but with this, it’s 5-6 minutes tops. It is very simple to use- once you plug the brush in it heats right up to temperature within seconds and is ready to go. It also, this comes with a comb that can help hold the hair in place while using the brush, but I have not had any need to use it. I simply section off my hair in four large segments and run the brush through each segment 2-3 times and I am done. The brush also cools down quickly after you turn it off, which is great for traveling purposes. Overall, I love the brush, and I get the results I want very quickly and require less time fixing my hair and exposing it to as much heat. text:
I use this liquid latex for nail art and it is FANTASTIC!!!! Some of the things you want in a latex product for nail art are that it dries quickly, leaves no staining on the skin, spreads easily and comes off in one piece. Another good thing would be an indicator that it is dry. This liquid latex does ALL of that. The purple gets darker as it dries. It is dry in like a minute as long as you don't apply globs of it, it spreads like a thin polish and it does not stain the skin.

I got this for my nail art in white originally and the white did not change color much and it was a little confusing since it was applied around a white base coat at times. This is like 100 times better and my favorite color. :-)

Four ounces will last you forever if you're using it for nail art. I usually use a children’s medicine syringe to put this into an old polish bottle that closes well, if not your latex will dry up in the bottle. If the brush gets gunky, I just use a toothpick or an orange wood stick to remove the gunk and it leaves the brush like new. If you quickly wash the syringe with water, you will be able to use it again and again.

I will definitely be a lifer with this product for my nail art. Pictured below is the water and hairspray method and you can see how much polish this kept off of my skin! Love it!

text:
To preface, I bought this product for when my hands or feet randomly get dry, and it seems I'll have to keep searching for a better product. Ultimately, I discarded this product. I do not recommend this because it did not moisturize my hands or feet. TIP: The best way to know if a product is good for your skin is to use it on your hands, feet, and/or face, since those are the most sensitive and picky areas (skin-wise) of the body. It dried out my hands and feet quickly after use, so I knew it would have the same effect on the rest of my body even if the effect is not as noticeable in other areas.

I tried to use this product when my hands or feet got dry. I like being in cold environments, so I often have lotion or something on standby for when my hands or feet inevitably get dry. Whenever I used this, my hands became dry again after about 30 minutes, when I expect it to last at least an hour like other products I've tried. So I decided this moisturizer is not for me and I ended up throwing this away.

Environmental considerations aside, I did not mind the glitter in the product. I just wish they focused more on the product actually working vs it looking pretty upon application.
